title: When the Fajitas Come Out Sizzling
about: When the Fajitas Come Out Sizzling is a catchphrase used in a series of memes, including image macros, conveying the feeling of when a person orders fajitas and they come out sizzling hot, often portrayed as a positive thing. Some versions of the meme specifically refer to the distracting nature of the sizzling when a plate of fajitas comes out of the kitchen at a restaurant. Memes about sizzling fajitas first started going viral online in 2018 and became increasingly popularized throughout the 2020s. One of the most viral versions of the meme is by Instagram user Cyrakek and features the caption, "When them fajitas come out sizzlin'," over a low-res image of Link from Good Mythical Morning dancing.
origin:
On August 29th, 2018, Redditor u/chainscriptbaby posted a meme to the /r/TooAfraidToAsk[1] subreddit captioned, "The entire restaurant when somebody order fajitas," showing a woman looking backward from her food at the distracting sizzling noise, garnering over 3,700 upvotes in seven years.

This is the earliest known meme about sizzling fajitas and was created by a social media user named mister_impossible, who posted it on an unknown date.

On May 16th, 2019, Facebook[2] user George Lopez posted a meme by a now-suspended X user, @CHASEEEZY, captioned, "Me when somebody orders fajitas and them shits come out sizzling," using the SpongeBob SquarePants Fish Turns Around meme to convey a distracted feeling, garnering over 5,600 reactions and 2,600 shares in six years.
